course_title: Applied Research in Criminology and Criminal Justice 2

learning_outcomes.description: Critically understand the rationale and processes for the collection, coding and analysis of qualitative and quantitative data
Demonstrate ability to conduct data analyses relevant to your research questions and aims
Understand and address ethical issues relating to social research as they arise through your research analysis and research translation
Demonstrate ability to interpret, synthesise and convincingly communicate your research evidence to your target audience(s)
Demonstrate ability to work effectively in a team to achieve project outcomes and outputs
Reflect critically on your learning and achievements to promote your employability

assessments.due_date:
Part A - Resume due: 17/08/2026 10:00 am

Part B - In-person practical (rostered time only) from: 17/08/2026 10:00 am

Part A Resume: Your Resume is due at 10AM on the date of your interview, via the dedicated Turnitin link on Blackboard.

Part B Interview: Your mock interview will be conducted in-person at a rostered time during extended class hours (between 10AM and 2PM). Your interview time will be posted in the week before your interview. You may bring your resume to the interview but no other notes or materials.
assessments.task_description:
In this assessment, you will reflect on your own skills, knowledge and attributes as you respond to a sample CJS related position description.

You will provide a 2-page Professional Resume that targets the position criteria and participate in an in-class mock interview for the position. You will articulate your professional identity and provide evidence of your suitability to the position through a two-page resume (submitted via Turnitin) and a professional pitch and interview (in-person). Further task details and requirements (including relevant marking criteria) can be found in the relevant Assessment folder on Blackboard. 

Submission: This assessment includes an in-person practical (mock interview and pitch) and two-page resume. You must attend your mock interview at the scheduled rostered time. You may refer to your resume during the interview.

Please refer to the submission instructions below for both Part A and Part B.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
14/09/2026 10:00 am

Please note that the Methods Defence is an individual in-person assessment. The Methods Defence will be conducted in-person at a rostered time during extended class hours this week (10AM-2PM). The rostered time will be posted the week before the Methods Defence. No written submission is required for this assessment.
assessments.task_description:
The Methods Defence Assessment is an opportunity for you to individually demonstrate your understanding of research methods and respective analytic approaches to your current research project. This task aims to strengthen capability to effectively engage with both qualitative and quantitative research methods by the conclusion of the course. 

In this in-person individual practical, you will demonstrate critical understanding of your methodological choices through structured questioning that explores rationale, implementation, analysis, and limitations. The assessment also requires you to articulate complex methodological concepts and justify decisions against alternatives while acknowledging methodological constraints.

Marking criteria: Criteria including rubrics are available in the ‘Assessments’ folder on the course Blackboard site.

AI Use: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. A single sided page of notes (A4) can be used during this assignment to sparingly prompt/reference specific elements or evidence. Successful completion of this assessment will require you to (independent of notes) understand your research methods and critically engage in a discussion about your project aims, methodological approach (including limitations, benefits and feasibility) and an exploration of alternative methods. 

A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct Policy. Additional resources are available through UQ AI Student Hub.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assignment is an individual in-person assessment. Students are not required to submit work to Blackboard for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Research Showcase Presentation and Research Brief (Group Assessment)
assessments.due_date:
Part A - PowerPoint and Brief: due 29/10/2026 1:00 pm

Part B - Research Showcase Presentation: commences 30/10/2026 11:00 am
assessments.task_description:
The Research Showcase is an opportunity for you to disseminate research evidence to industry, staff and peers. As a group, use your research activities, brief, and developing report as the foundation for your 2-page brief/executive summary (Part A) and presentation (Part B). Both should cover your research problem, aims and questions, methods, results, and conclusions.

The presentation and brief should be visually engaging and demonstrate expertise in the topic area. These outputs should communicate content clearly and appropriately for a non-specialist audience and use persuasive language that conveys the significance of the research and research findings.

Further task details and requirements (including relevant marking criteria) can be found in the relevant Assessment folder on blackboard.

Please ensure that all sources are authentic and appropriately evidenced in the brief and presentation, including the use of AI.

AI Use: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI may provide limited support and guidance. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct Policy. Students may be required to complete an AI usage declaration and submit it with their assessment. Additional resources are available through UQ AI Student Hub.


SUBMISSION:

  • Submit Part A - Research Showcase PowerPoint Presentation and Research Brief to Turnitin by 1PM, Thursday 29th October to enable consolidation (Presentation PowerPoints) and printing (Briefs)
  • One submission per group.
  • Part B - Research Showcase Presentations will occur in person on Friday 30th October from 11AM to 2PM. Printed copies of your Research Briefs will be available to showcase attendees.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.weighting: <dd>35% Report + PAF + Project Progress Tasks x 10 </dd>
assessments.task_description:
Final Report

The Final Project Report is the main product of the research team’s efforts over the course of the year. The Final Report is an opportunity for your team to demonstrate your research evaluation skills and knowledge acquired through your applied industry research project. The Final Report should be well structured and written to a high standard. The Final Report (6000-7000 words) will include an introduction to the research problem, aims and questions, literature, key concepts or theoretical framework, methodology, results, discussion and conclusion (with implications). All literature, sources and data must be authentic, verifiable, appropriately evidenced and accurately referenced including use of AI tools. Further task details and requirements (including relevant marking criteria) can be found in the relevant Assessment folder on Blackboard.

AI Use: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI may provide limited support and guidance. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct Policy. Students may be required to complete an AI usage declaration and submit it with their assessment. Additional resources are available through UQ AI Student Hub.


Project Progress Tasks

Each team member is required to complete a weekly Project Progress Task (weeks 1-12, excluding 4,8,13) to document their specific contributions, commitment to deliverables for the following week, and to assess group progress. The Project Progress Tasks are to be completed via a Qualtrics survey link in class or MS Teams as relevant for the specific week.

Your contribution to the Project Progress Tasks will be assessed by the course coordinator during your in-class project work and through the contributions you make on MS Teams during weeks 1-12 (excluding assessment weeks). Each project progress task is worth .5 points for a total of 5 points towards your individual report mark.

Grading Impact: Individual final marks will be calculated as: Group Project Mark (max 30 points) + .5 x each completed project progress task x 10 (= max 5 points) x PAF (via Buddycheck) = Individual Mark (max 35 points).

Buddycheck: This assessment includes a Buddycheck. Each team member will complete a Buddycheck form by the due date which will provide an opportunity to reflect on the contribution each member of the team has made to the WHOLE group project. The Buddycheck form may be used to adjust marks according to contribution.
assessments.submission_guidelines: For the Final Report, only one member of your group is required to submit your assignment electronically by the due date and time.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
